comments.author: @WhereonEarth4K
comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Oman episode. Tours, books, cookbooks, travel resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE OMAN COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we'd recommend for this episode → https://amzn.to/4uDUBkc

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN OMAN*
  ◦ Wahiba Sands desert, Musandam fjords, Salalah, Nizwa fort, Wadi Shab → https://shorturl.at/85dUp
  ◦ Browse all Oman experiences → https://shorturl.at/vbDuW

*📖 PLAN YOUR OMAN TRIP*
  ◦ Bradt Oman, 5th edition (the only dedicated English-language guide to Oman) → https://amzn.to/49M7MaH
  ◦ Lonely Planet Middle East Phrasebook (covers Gulf Arabic spoken in Oman) → https://amzn.to/4fMRgL8

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO OMAN*
  ◦ A Sea of Wealth by Nicholas Roberts (the authoritative history of the Omani Empire from Hormuz to Zanzibar) → https://amzn.to/4uvVtr8
  ◦ Arabian Sands by Wilfred Thesiger (the classic account of crossing the Empty Quarter with Omani Bedu) → https://amzn.to/4e9Vifl

*🍲 BRING OMAN HOME*
  ◦ Bahari by Dina Macki (the first Omani cookbook written by an Omani chef) → https://amzn.to/49mIqzW
  ◦ Oman Odyssey by Assouline (Oliver Pilcher's new coffee-table photography book on Oman) → https://amzn.to/4u2NZL8

comments.text: I don't trust tribal societies, including Oman, which is governed by absolute, hereditary monarchy

comments.author: @balkan_trip
comments.text: The only peaceful muslim country...

comments.author: @4249wrja
comments.text: I lived for a year in Oman. Kindly lovely people. I felt welcome. My understanding at the time was there were about 2 million people and a proportion of them, mostly workers, came from Karachi. It is most unhappy that Iran would even think of bombing the only country that is as neutral as Oman like Switzerland. Thoughtless. Wonderful account here.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: A year on the ground gives you what no documentary can — that texture of daily life and hospitality that only comes through in person. The Karachi thread is one of those layers the film could only gesture at. What surprised you most about Oman during that year?

comments.author: @Robert-g5v9d
comments.text: The jcpoa was wishful thinking, nothing more. Iran has never given up their efforts for a nuclear weapon and probably never will!

comments.author: @Ammani-Salti-48
comments.text: Thank you  for this documentory, around minute 30 you show a bridge linking Asia and Europe... It 's in Istanbul, not Oman. Also a few times you showed a grand white Mosque... This is in Dubai, not Oman, so it is misleading. Correct me if I'm wrong.

comments.author: @BestTravel2
comments.text: Oman looks absolutely stunning here. The landscapes, the light, the atmosphere… everything feels calm and untouched. The way the story is told really pulls you in and makes the whole place feel even more special.
